title: Podman REST API - context/files not found for building image
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
This is related to issue: [https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11261](url) unfortunately I couldn't reopen this

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. A .tar package containing all files incl. the Dockerfile is created and stored on a remote-repository (e.g. github)
    Jenkins downloads the .tar file to a temporally directory.
    `curl -L https://github.com/blindshare/simpleCherryPyPage/tarball/master > /data/work/simpletest/simpleTest.tar`

2. Jenkins uses the the following curl statement to upload the .tar to the podman "Server" and tries to build a new image:
    `curl -X POST --header Content-Type: application/x-tar --data-binary @/data/work/simpletest/simpleTest.tar -v http://192.168.0.3:2345/v3.0.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=blindshare-simpleCherryPyPage-64f9421/Dockerfile`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
dir inside tar is: blindshare-simpleCherryPyPage-64f9421/

[Pipeline] }
[Pipeline] // script
[Pipeline] }
[Pipeline] // stage
[Pipeline] stage
[Pipeline] { (build image)
[Pipeline] echo
build container
[Pipeline] script
[Pipeline] {
[Pipeline] sh
+ curl -X POST --header Content-Type: application/x-tar --data-binary @/data/work/simpletest/simpleTest.tar -v http://192.168.0.3:2345/v3.0.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=blindshare-simpleCherryPyPage-64f9421/
Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, POST is already inferred.

* TCP_NODELAY set

{"stream":"Successfully installed cheroot-8.5.2 cherrypy-18.6.1 jaraco.classes-3.2.1 jaraco.collections-3.4.0 jaraco.functools-3.3.0 jaraco.text-3.5.1 more-itertools-8.8.0 portend-2.7.1 pytz-2021.1 tempora-4.1.1 zc.lockfile-2.0\n"}
{"stream":"STEP 7: RUN  mkdir /opt/simpleTest\n"}
{"stream":"STEP 8: COPY simple.py /opt/simpleTest\n"}
{"error":"error building at STEP \"COPY simple.py /opt/simpleTest\": checking on sources under \"/var/tmp/libpod_builder436755164/build\": copier: stat: \"/simple.py\": no such file or directory\n"}
+ Dockerfile
/var/jenkins_home/workspace/simpletest - local docker@tmp/durable-804b18dc/script.sh: 2: /var/jenkins_home/workspace/simpletest - local docker@tmp/durable-804b18dc/script.sh: Dockerfile: not found
[Pipeline] }
[Pipeline] // script
[Pipeline] }
[Pipeline] // stage
[Pipeline] stage
[Pipeline] { (run container)
Stage "run container" skipped due to earlier failure(s)
[Pipeline] }
[Pipeline] // stage
[Pipeline] }
[Pipeline] // node
[Pipeline] End of Pipeline
ERROR: script returned exit code 127
Finished: FAILURE
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

It seems that podman can't find the files stored in the .tar file ... but found the including "Dockerfile". 
(No previous manual step unpacks the .tar)
Does "build?dockerfile" only extracts the Dockerfile but not the rest ? And if so, how to set the "context" ?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

comments.body: Can you retest with v3.3.0 (just released)? We made a number of improvements to the build API during the new release.
Unfortunately not. There are no packages available for Ubuntu nor RHEL atm.

Ok, i tried with main branch and docker.  the behaviors are the same (failing to copy the python file).  I believe this is because while the Dockerfile is properly given, the context dir is not being set to the same dir as to where the Dockerfile is.  therefore, i believe to make this work, you need to provide a path to the python file based on the context dir.  I think this should be simpletest/simple.py  @jwhonce wdyt?

I don't know ... thing is: I'm sending a .tar file which includes everything. And since the process is able to extract/process the Dockerfile inside the archive I'd presume it can/does take the .tar file as "Context".

I checked the content of /var/tmp ... I can see that all files are extracted here (temporally). 
```
$ watch ls -R /var/tmp
total 24
drwx--x--x 4 martin martin 4096 Aug 24 11:12 buildah778147622

```

So all files ar present, but the "Context-Dir" is not set

IMO the "remote" endpoint should be used here (.../libpod/build?remote=...), but then neither the .tar nor any other file gets found:

```
remote

string A Git repository URI or HTTP/HTTPS context URI. 
If the URI points to a single text file, the file’s contents are placed into a file called Dockerfile and the image is built from that file. 
If the URI points to a tarball, the file is downloaded by the daemon and the contents therein used as the context for the build. 
If the URI points to a tarball and the dockerfile parameter is also specified, there must be a file with the corresponding path inside the tarball. (As of version 1.xx)
```

```
curl -X POST --header Content-Type: application/x-tar --data-binary @/data/work/simpletest/simpleTest.tar -v http://192.168.0.3:2345/v3.0.0/libpod/build?remote=simpleTest.tar&dockerfile=blindshare-simpleCherryPyPage-fa80160

```
curl -X POST --header Content-Type: application/x-tar --data-binary @/data/work/simpletest/simpleTest.tar -v http://192.168.0.3:2345/v3.0.0/libpod/build?dockerfile=/data/work/simpletest/blindshare-simpleCherryPyPage-fa80160

* Connection #1 to host 192.168.0.3 left intact
```

I believe this is the exact behavior as docker.  And while you are specifically hitting a libpod endpoint, we generally do not change behaviors like that.  If I am wrong about the docker claim, then we should fix that.  But, when I tested your tar with Docker earlier this week, I got the same results and came to the same conclusion.

@baude 
so, there is no possibility to "add" the subdir from the .tar file to the Context-Dir ? In other words, your tarball must have all files at its "root" ? 
But if so, why can I specify a different context for "dockerfile" ? (see above) That doesn't make sense. 
Podman would find the Dockerfile - start processing it - but could never include any external files as its context-dir can not be set/extended.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

In interest of keeping parity with docker engine api we cannot change build context on `param: build?dockerfile` hence closing this as `WONTFIX`. If you think this is something which will be useful please open `RFE` and we can discuss new parameter there.
